title: GLM-5
description: A large open-weights GLM model that pairs sparse-attention and mixture-of-experts routing with an agentic-engineering training story.
openingSummary: GLM-5 matters because it is the first public GLM checkpoint framed around agentic engineering rather than casual code completion. The technical report positions the model as a sparse-attention, mixture-of-experts transformer with open weights, a long context window, and training choices aimed at sustained tool use and multi-step work.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: GLM-5 is an open-weights text model from Z.ai, released on 2026-02-17 in the GLM-5 technical report. It is a large sparse mixture-of-experts transformer advertised with 744 billion total parameters and about 40 billion active parameters per token.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: GLM-5 is a text-in, text-out model. The public release emphasizes long prompts, coding, and agentic workflows where the model must keep working across many tool calls instead of stopping after one short answer.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: At a high level, GLM-5 keeps a decoder-only transformer skeleton but replaces dense attention and feed-forward paths with sparse attention and routed experts. The report highlights DSA-style sparse attention, mixture-of-experts blocks, multi-head latent attention for cache efficiency, RMSNorm, and RoPE as the named building blocks.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: These modules explain why GLM-5 is grouped with modern long-context MoE stacks rather than a plain dense transformer. Sparse attention and mixture-of-experts carry most of the scaling story, while latent attention, RMSNorm, and RoPE support stable training and long prompts.

training.title: Training
training.body: The GLM-5 report frames training around large-scale pretraining followed by post-training aimed at agentic engineering: sustained coding, tool use, and multi-step task completion rather than one-shot chat. The public materials describe open-weight release in bf16 rather than a closed API-only surface.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: GLM-5 advertises a 202,752-token context window in public model-card material. That is long enough for repository-scale context, but the later GLM-5.2 release pushes context and coding emphasis further. Read GLM-5 first for the original architecture and agentic-engineering story, then follow the paired model page for the newer line member.

model.glm-5-2.reason: GLM-5.2 is the later GLM line member that extends the same family with a longer advertised context window and refreshed coding emphasis.

architectureGraph.alt: GLM-5 architecture diagram with token embeddings, RoPE position encoding, a repeated decoder block containing sparse attention, RMSNorm, and mixture-of-experts routing, then an output projection and softmax.

outputProbabilities.label:
Output
Probabilities
outputProbabilities.summary: The model's probability distribution over the next token

softmax.label: Softmax
softmax.summary: Turns final vocabulary scores into probabilities

linear.label:
Output
Linear
linear.summary: Projects the final hidden state into vocabulary logits

decoderStack.summary: Repeated decoder block container for the main GLM-5 stack

repeatMarker.label: N×
repeatMarker.summary: This decoder block repeats through the full stack

rmsnormAttention.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormAttention.summary: Normalizes the residual stream before the sparse attention step

sparseAttention.label:
Sparse
Attention
(DSA)
sparseAttention.summary: The attention path uses DSA-style sparsity to cut long-context cost while keeping useful global context

rmsnormMoe.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormMoe.summary: Normalizes the stream before the sparse expert block

mixture-of-experts.label:
Mixture
Of Experts
mixture-of-experts.summary: Routes each token through a small active set of experts instead of one dense feed-forward path

ropeInject.summary: Applies token states together with rotary position information before the stack

rope.label: RoPE
rope.summary: Rotary position encoding gives the attention stack token order information

inputEmbedding.label:
Input
Embedding
inputEmbedding.summary: Maps token IDs into hidden vectors
